name: Partstable IT Hardware Parts MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from Partstable (partstable org) for IT hardware parts research. Enables AI agents to normalize part numbers, search hardware component listings, find substitutes and compatible alternatives, and compare IT hardware inventory data — streamlining hardware procurement and compatibility research workflows.
use_cases: Researching IT hardware components and compatibility from procurement agents
Finding substitute or compatible parts for out-of-stock components from supply chain agents
Normalizing part numbers across vendor catalogs from inventory management agents
Comparing hardware listings and pricing across sources from purchasing agents
not_for: Consumer electronics (focused on IT/enterprise hardware components)
Software licensing or cloud services procurement
Real-time pricing (check Partstable for actual pricing feature support)
best_when: An IT procurement or supply chain agent needs to research hardware parts — finding components, checking compatibility, identifying substitutes, and normalizing part numbers across vendors.
avoid_when: You don't work with IT hardware procurement. Partstable is specifically for hardware component research.

auth.notes: Partstable API key required. Register at partstable.com for access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier for limited queries; check partstable.com for current limits
pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans for higher search volume
pricing.notes: Free tier available. MCP server is free open source from partstable org.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits by tier; check documentation

known_agent_gotchas: Database coverage varies by vendor and region — test coverage for your specific hardware suppliers
Part number normalization is a hard problem — verify output against vendor catalogs for critical procurement
Official from partstable org — purpose-built for IT hardware parts research
Pricing data may not be real-time — verify current pricing directly with vendors for procurement decisions
agent_readiness.security_notes: Hardware procurement data — non-sensitive. API key auth. HTTPS. No PII in typical hardware search queries.

security_score_components.security_notes: Non-sensitive hardware data. API key auth. HTTPS. Low security risk use case.
